qa_pairs.question: What are the distinctions between `StdEnv/2023` and the preceding standard environments?
qa_pairs.answer: The differences are discussed in the 'Standard software environments' document.

qa_pairs.question: When will `StdEnv/2023` become the default environment for all clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: `StdEnv/2023` will become the default environment for all clusters after April 1, 2024.

qa_pairs.question: How can I set a custom default standard environment for my account?
qa_pairs.answer: You can modify your `$HOME/.modulerc` file by adding a line like `module-version StdEnv/2020 default`, and then logging out and logging in again for the change to take effect.

qa_pairs.question: Is it necessary to recompile my code or reinstall packages when the standard environment changes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you compile your own code or have installed R or Python packages, you should recompile your code or reinstall the packages with the newest version of the standard environment.

qa_pairs.question: What is the method for using an earlier standard environment in a job script?
qa_pairs.answer: To use an earlier environment, add `module load StdEnv/2020` (or your desired version) to your job scripts before loading any other modules.

qa_pairs.question: Will previous standard environments be removed, or will they remain accessible?
qa_pairs.answer: Earlier environments and their dependent software will remain available, but versions `2016.4` and `2018.3` are no longer supported and are not recommended; staff will only install software in `StdEnv/2023`.

qa_pairs.question: Can I combine modules from different standard environments within a single job?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you should use a single standard environment for a given job, as attempting to mix different environments typically leads to unpredictable errors.

qa_pairs.question: Which standard environment should I utilize for new projects or to access the latest application versions?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are starting a new project or want to use a newer version of an application, you should use `StdEnv/2023` by adding `module load StdEnv/2023` to your job scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Is it recommended to load modules in my `.bashrc` file to maintain an older environment?
qa_pairs.answer: No, loading modules in your `.bashrc` is not recommended; instead, explicitly load modules in your job scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Does this standard environment change affect users who only use cloud resources, not HPC clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, this change only affects the available software accessed by using environment modules on HPC clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if a module I previously used is no longer available to load?
qa_pairs.answer: Newer versions of most applications are installed in the new environment; you can use the `module avail <application>` command to see the available versions.
